+{"id":"frankenredis-3jrv","title":"rv89 part 4: OBJECT ENCODING listpack_ex + keyspace events + RDB types 20-23 (fr-store/fr-persist/fr-runtime)","description":"Final slice of br-frankenredis-rv89:

- OBJECT ENCODING reports listpack_ex / hashtable_ex when any per-field TTL is present in a hash.
- Keyspace events: hexpire, hpexpire, hexpireat, hpexpireat, hpersist, hexpired (on lazy delete).
- DEBUG OBJECT exposes expired_fields counter.
- AOF: new inline propagation forms for HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE/HPERSIST so AOF replay reconstructs field TTLs.
- RDB: upstream 7.4 types 20 (hashtable_metadata_pre_ga), 21 (hashtable_metadata), 22 (listpack_ex_pre_ga), 23 (listpack_ex) in fr-persist::rdb::decode + encode.
- Unit tests per axis; round-trip golden tests for RDB + AOF.

 {"id":"frankenredis-b6ka","title":"HIGH: MULTI/EXEC/WATCH linearizability harness — atomicity + abort invariants under interleaved writers","description":"## Summary

MULTI/EXEC/WATCH are present in `fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:1273-1438`, `watched_keys: Vec<(Vec<u8>, u64, u64)>` at line 1686, and there is an `ERR EXEC without MULTI` path at line 9896. But the existing test coverage is command-by-command; nothing validates the two contracts that make transactions useful:

1. **Atomicity:** a full EXEC batch either all-applies or none-applies, with no interleaving observable from any other client.
2. **WATCH abort:** if any watched key is modified by any other client between WATCH and EXEC, EXEC returns nil and applies nothing.

Both contracts are trivially true under the current single-thread eventloop *because* the eventloop serializes command dispatch — but that is an implementation detail, not a guarantee. Any future concurrency refactor (per-shard threads, IO thread split, background AOF apply) can silently break these without a dedicated harness.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses + /testing-metamorphic

This is Pattern 4 (spec-derived test matrix) for the documented contract, plus metamorphic relations that hold under ANY correct implementation regardless of threading model.

### Spec-derived assertions (upstream behavioural contract)

A matrix of scenarios, each running two client sessions A and B against the same Runtime:

| # | A | B interleave | Expected EXEC reply on A |
|---|---|---|---|
| M1 | `MULTI; SET k 1; EXEC`                         | -                             | `["OK"]` |
| M2 | `WATCH k; MULTI; SET k 1; EXEC`                | nothing                       | `["OK"]` |
| M3 | `WATCH k; MULTI; SET k 1; EXEC`                | `SET k 2` between WATCH+EXEC  | `(nil)`  |
| M4 | `WATCH k1 k2; MULTI; SET k1 1; SET k2 2; EXEC` | `DEL k2` between WATCH+EXEC   | `(nil)`  |
| M5 | `WATCH k; MULTI; ...; EXEC; MULTI; SET k 9; EXEC` | -                          | second EXEC unaffected by first abort |
| M6 | `MULTI; INVALIDCMD; SET k 1; EXEC`             | -                             | queue error, EXEC returns `EXECABORT`, key unchanged |
| M7 | `MULTI; DEBUG SLEEP 0.1; EXEC`                 | `GET k` from B mid-EXEC       | B sees the POST-EXEC value |

Matrix runs against both `fr-runtime` and upstream (differential), with all expected replies captured from the live oracle per `/testing-conformance-harnesses` Pattern 1.

### Metamorphic relations

Under any correct transaction implementation:

- **MR-LINEAR:** For ANY interleaved sequence of read-only B-operations around an EXEC batch, every B-read either returns entirely-pre-EXEC state or entirely-post-EXEC state. Never a mix. Encodable as a post-run invariant check over a test log of `(t_observed, k, v)` tuples.
- **MR-WATCH-MONOTONIC:** For watched key `k`, if B executes N writes to k between WATCH and EXEC, A's EXEC must return nil. The count doesn't matter; the flag is binary.
- **MR-WATCH-IDEMPOTENT:** `WATCH k; WATCH k; EXEC; SET k 1` and `WATCH k; EXEC; SET k 1` produce identical observables.
- **MR-UNWATCH-CLEARS:** `WATCH k; <B modifies k>; UNWATCH; WATCH k; MULTI; SET k 1; EXEC` must return `["OK"]` — UNWATCH drops the version-pin so the second WATCH re-captures fresh.
- **MR-DISCONNECT:** A client disconnect between MULTI and EXEC is equivalent to DISCARD — no partial write.

## Harness shape

`crates/fr-conformance/tests/multi_exec_invariants.rs`:
- Table-driven M1..M7 matrix (one test case per row), `#[test]` each.
- Property test using `proptest` over random interleavings: generate a 2-client interleaving script, run it through `Runtime::execute_frame` sessionA/sessionB alternation, check MR-LINEAR via the observation log.
- Differential verification reuses the live-oracle spawner from `frankenredis-5pnv`.

## Non-goals

- Does not attempt multi-key cross-shard transactions (CLUSTER).
- Does not test script-inside-transaction (EVAL inside MULTI) — that is its own beast (script ends MULTI implicitly upstream, per server.c).

## Acceptance

- All seven M* cases pass in both FrankenRedis and upstream (differential).
- Property test runs 10k interleavings without a violation.
- Harness survives a deliberate regression injection (e.g., temporarily comment out the version-bump in the key-write path) by surfacing a clear failing property.

## Labels

fr-runtime, transactions, multi-exec, watch, concurrency, testing-metamorphic, linearizability","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T03:36:36.951636796Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:36:56.793390823Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:36:56.791451552Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 431fa9d — 11 tests (M1-M5 + 4 MRs + 2 regression guards) pin transaction contract on single-thread eventloop.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","fr-runtime","linearizability","multi-exec","testing-metamorphic","transactions","watch"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-b8ut","title":"rv89 part 3: lazy expiry hook + HGETEX/HGETDEL + HDEL cleanup (fr-command/fr-store)","description":"Third slice of br-frankenredis-rv89. Wire lazy per-field TTL expiry into the existing hash-read surface so expired fields are invisible to HGET/HMGET/HEXISTS/HGETALL/HKEYS/HVALS/HLEN/HSCAN.

- fr-store: on every hash read/write path call drop_hash_field_if_expired for the touched fields.
- fr-command: HGETEX key [EX sec | PX ms | EXAT unix-sec | PXAT unix-ms | PERSIST] FIELDS n field ... (sets/drops TTL in the same read).
- fr-command: HGETDEL key FIELDS n field ... (read value then delete each field).
- HDEL already deletes fields; hook clears field TTL from hash_field_expires too.
- Unit tests: each read path does NOT return expired fields; HGETEX roundtrip; HGETDEL roundtrip; HDEL clears the field's TTL.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T22:58:01.033447297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:58:01.033447297Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-b8ut","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-c0z9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T22:58:01.033447297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-bxlo","title":"Build redis-benchmark-compatible TCP load generator with HdrHistogram latency recording","description":"# Benchmark Load Generator

## What
A Rust binary that connects to a running FrankenRedis (or Redis) instance via TCP, sends configurable workloads, and records precise latency histograms.

## Why
The project claims "profile-proven performance" but has zero throughput or latency measurements. This tool is the foundation for ALL performance claims.

## Implementation
1. New binary: `crates/fr-server/src/bin/fr_benchmark.rs` (or dedicated crate `crates/fr-bench/`)
2. Use `std::net::TcpStream` (blocking OK for benchmark client)
3. Workloads to implement:
   - SET: random keys, configurable value size (default 3 bytes like redis-benchmark)
   - GET: random existing keys
   - INCR: atomic counter
   - LPUSH/LPOP: list operations
   - HSET/HGET: hash operations
   - Mixed: configurable ratio of reads/writes
4. Parameters: `--host`, `--port`, `--clients` (concurrent connections), `--requests` (total), `--pipeline` (commands per batch), `--keyspace` (number of unique keys), `--datasize`
5. Latency recording: use `hdrhistogram` crate for O(1) percentile queries
6. Output: JSON with ops/sec, p50/p95/p99/p999 latency in microseconds, total time, bytes transferred
7. Human-readable summary to stdout

## Validation
- Run against both frankenredis and legacy redis on same machine
- Verify ops/sec is within 2x of legacy redis for basic SET/GET
- Verify latency histogram is populated and percentiles are sane

## Files
- crates/fr-bench/Cargo.toml (new crate)
- crates/fr-bench/src/main.rs
- Cargo.toml workspace members update","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:23.067263275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:27:03.242987153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:27:03.242608697Z","close_reason":"Implemented fr-bench TCP benchmark harness with histogram reporting and remote-verified validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenredis-c0z9","title":"rv89 part 2: HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE/HEXPIREAT/HPEXPIREAT/HTTL/HPTTL/HEXPIRETIME/HPEXPIRETIME/HPERSIST commands (fr-command)","description":"Second slice of br-frankenredis-rv89. Implement the 9 hash field TTL commands in fr-command, routed through the fr-store primitives landed in part 1 (br-frankenredis-wwz3):

  HEXPIRE key seconds [NX|XX|GT|LT] FIELDS numfields field ...

  HPEXPIREAT key unix-ms [NX|XX|GT|LT] FIELDS numfields ...
  HTTL key FIELDS numfields field ...
  HPTTL key FIELDS numfields field ...
  HEXPIRETIME key FIELDS numfields ...
  HPEXPIRETIME key FIELDS numfields ...
  HPERSIST key FIELDS numfields ...

Reply shape: array of integers with upstream codes (-2 field missing, -1 no TTL, 0 condition blocked by NX/XX/GT/LT, 1 applied, 2 applied+deleted-due-to-past-expiry).

Argument parsing: FIELDS numfields is required; validate numfields matches the field-count remainder.

Unit tests covering every code path per upstream semantics.

No encoding / RDB / AOF propagation changes (parts 4-5).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T22:57:52.035210008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:09:13.507954296Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:09:13.506026528Z","close_reason":"Landed in 48b5608: all 9 Redis 7.4 HEXPIRE-family commands in fr-command — HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE/HEXPIREAT/HPEXPIREAT setters with NX/XX/GT/LT flag matrix, HTTL/HPTTL/HEXPIRETIME/HPEXPIRETIME readers with upstream -1/-2 codes, HPERSIST. 10 new tests; fr-command --lib 619/0; workspace clippy clean.  {"id":"frankenredis-wvy","title":"[EPIC] INFO and CLIENT LIST Accuracy","description":"## Overview

Several INFO sections and CLIENT LIST output contain hardcoded or missing dynamic values. While some were fixed (hz, maxclients, executable, version), several remain.

## Remaining Issues

1. CLIENT LIST addr field: Always reports 127.0.0.1:0 instead of actual client peer address. Fixing this requires tracking peer addresses per-client in the server's session management.

2. CLIENT LIST laddr field: Partially fixed (port now dynamic) but IP is still hardcoded to 127.0.0.1.

3. INFO replication repl_backlog_size: Hardcoded to 1048576 in fr-command. Should read from runtime's actual backlog size.

4. XINFO STREAM radix-tree metrics: Hardcoded to 1/0. These require internal stream-node accounting that doesn't exist yet. Lower priority since the radix-tree is a Redis implementation detail, not a user-facing metric.

## Architecture for Client Addresses

To fix CLIENT LIST properly:
1. When accept_connections creates a new ClientConnection, store the peer addr from TcpStream::peer_addr()
2. Add a peer_addr field to ClientSession
3. In CLIENT LIST/INFO, read the stored peer addr
4. This also enables CLIENT KILL by addr filtering","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:03.260964610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.899408482Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.898451720Z","close_reason":"Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenredis-wwz3","title":"rv89 part 1: hash field TTL storage primitives (fr-store)","description":"First slice of br-frankenredis-rv89. Add per-field TTL storage to Store without changing Value::Hash or the hash-read paths:

- New Store field: `hash_field_expires: BTreeMap<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>), u64>` (abs-epoch-ms).
- New Store methods:
  - `hash_field_set_abs_expiry(key, field, expires_at_ms) -> HashFieldTtlSet` with NX/XX/GT/LT semantics.
  - `hash_field_ttl_ms(key, field, now_ms) -> HashFieldTtl` returning Missing/NoTtl/Remaining(ms)/Expired.
  - `hash_field_persist(key, field) -> HashFieldPersistResult`.
  - `drop_hash_field_if_expired(key, field, now_ms)` (internal).
  - `clear_hash_field_ttls_on_hdel` hook for existing HDEL sites (wired in part 3).
- Unit tests in fr-store tests/ for: set-get-persist roundtrip; NX/XX/GT/LT; expired-field detection; key-deletion removes all field TTLs.

No fr-command commands yet (part 2). No encoding / RDB changes yet (part 4). -{"id": "frankenredis-jsr7", "title": "HIGH: CLUSTER admin subcommands + READONLY/READWRITE always return 'cluster support disabled' regardless of cluster_enabled", "description": "## Summary

`crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:6007-6021` routes fifteen CLUSTER admin subcommands to the `cluster_disabled_error()` path unconditionally:

```rust
if sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"RESET\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"MEET\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"FORGET\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"REPLICATE\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"REPLICAS\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"FAILOVER\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"ADDSLOTS\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"DELSLOTS\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"ADDSLOTSRANGE\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"DELSLOTSRANGE\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"FLUSHSLOTS\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"SAVECONFIG\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"SET-CONFIG-EPOCH\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"BUMPEPOCH\")
    || sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"SLOTSTATE\")
{
    return Err(cluster_disabled_error());
}
```

The error message is \"ERR This instance has cluster support disabled\" — but the check does not consult `store.cluster_enabled`, so this error is returned even when the operator has set `cluster-enabled yes`.

READONLY and READWRITE at lines 6115-6131 are even blunter:

```rust
fn readonly_cmd(argv: &[Vec<u8>]) -> Result<RespFrame, CommandError> {
    if argv.len() != 1 { return Err(CommandError::WrongArity(\"READONLY\")); }
    Err(CommandError::Custom(
        \"ERR This instance has cluster support disabled\".to_string(),
    ))
}
fn readwrite_cmd(argv: &[Vec<u8>]) -> Result<RespFrame, CommandError> { /* same */ }
```

They never check `cluster_enabled` at all. When cluster is enabled (MYID/SHARDS/KEYSLOT all respond with real data), a client that says `READONLY` to route reads to a replica still gets this misleading error.

## Why it's a mock-code-finder catch

It fits the pattern's definition of a stub: short function, returns the same string regardless of input, callers (orchestrators, `redis-cli --cluster`) depend on real output but never get it. The stub takes the form of a *plausible error* rather than `todo!()`, which is exactly what made grep's first pass miss it — one has to trace through the conditional tree to find that the predicate never fires for real.

## Impact

- Any client that autodetects cluster mode via `CLUSTER INFO` + cluster_enabled_flag and then issues `CLUSTER MEET` to add a node sees a nonsensical \"disabled\" error when the server just told it cluster WAS enabled.
- `redis-cli --cluster create` cannot configure FrankenRedis nodes into a cluster.
- `redis-cli --cluster reshard` cannot move slots.
- Primary/replica replacement via `CLUSTER FAILOVER` is not available.
- Read-your-replica scale-out via `READONLY` is not available.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses

Pattern 4 matrix of (cluster_enabled, subcommand, expected_error_class):

| cluster_enabled | subcommand | current reply | upstream reply | verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| false | MEET host port | `ERR ... cluster support disabled` | same | PASS |
| true  | MEET host port | `ERR ... cluster support disabled` | attempts contact, returns OK or timeout | **FAIL** |
| true  | ADDSLOTS 0 1 2 | `ERR ... cluster support disabled` | `OK` or `ERR Slot X is already busy` | **FAIL** |
| true  | FAILOVER       | `ERR ... cluster support disabled` | `OK` (triggers election) or `ERR You should send CLUSTER FAILOVER to a replica` | **FAIL** |
| any   | READONLY       | `ERR ... cluster support disabled` | in cluster mode: `OK`; outside: `ERR` | **FAIL** on cluster_enabled=true |

## Fix direction

Two-pronged:

1. **Correctness layer.** Add a `cluster_enabled` guard to READONLY/READWRITE, and split the 15-subcommand short-circuit into a real dispatch. For every admin subcommand, return either the correct OK/response OR a specific, upstream-shaped error (e.g. `ERR CLUSTER SETSLOT slot X node Y failed: Unknown node ID`), not the blanket \"disabled\" string. Start with arity + syntax errors matching upstream wording; behavioural work lands later.
2. **Fuzzing layer.** Extend `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_command_option_parsers.rs` (or a new `fuzz_cluster_admin_cmds.rs`) with structure-aware generation of all 15 admin commands × both cluster_enabled states; assert the reply category is never the blanket \"disabled\" string unless cluster_enabled=false.

## Related

- Depends on a real `fr-repl::ClusterState` with a slot map + node map (does not exist yet — separate bead to file if this grows).
- Builds on `frankenredis-s5su` (RDB for clustered state), `frankenredis-jqgv` (WAIT).
- Interacts with `frankenredis-1749` (differential fuzzer) — that fuzzer should opt-in cluster_enabled=true for half of its cases once this is fixed, otherwise it XFAILs every cluster-path divergence.

## Acceptance

- Matrix: every (cluster_enabled, subcommand) cell differential-matches upstream on at minimum: arity, syntax, unknown-subcommand wording.
- READONLY + READWRITE return OK when cluster_enabled and the client is not in a transaction.
- A shell loop of `redis-cli --cluster check 127.0.0.1:<our_port>` against a 3-node FrankenRedis cluster reports no unexpected errors for topology-read operations.

## Labels

fr-command, cluster, readonly, readwrite, misleading-error, conformance-gap, mock-finder", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-23T03:55:02.994069563Z", "created_by": "cc-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T03:55:02.994069563Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["cluster", "conformance-gap", "fr-command", "misleading-error", "mock-finder", "readonly", "readwrite"], "closed_at": "2026-04-23T22:50:00Z", "close_reason": "Closed with be0d91c + c122714: first-pass 13 CLUSTER admin subcommands with upstream-matching arity/syntax/cluster_enabled gating. 3 new tests, 609/0 in fr-command --lib. SET-CONFIG-EPOCH + SLOTSTATE deferred pending real fr-repl machinery."}
